human_verification.notes: Reviewed healthcare operations boundaries, access/throughput/capacity/staffing/downtime artifacts, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation rules, research-source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for clinical judgment, payer interpretation, authorization approval, PHI disclosure, staffing licensure, downtime command ownership, and executive resource decisions.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers normal operations, capacity delay, missing prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical escalation, staffing/licensure escalation, downtime, source conflict, payer-authority refusal, policy conflict, tenant terminology, and completion evidence.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
